idea-plugin.name: JPA Fetch Lens
idea-plugin.description:
Hover over a Spring Data JPA repository method to see how far its effect reaches across your entity graph — shown as a color-coded tree right below the method's Java documentation. Read methods show what the query loads; save and delete show what cascades along with them. What the method name hides — N+1 risks, a LAZY field loaded eagerly anyway, or a delete that quietly removes children — becomes visible without leaving the editor.
  • Read methods — fetch tree: the queried entity and the associations that get loaded. Green = pulled by this query (join fetch / @EntityGraph), yellow = EAGER mapping, red = LAZY (proxy, potential N+1).
  • save — cascade tree: associations cascaded by PERSIST / MERGE are shown in blue and expanded; the rest are gray.
  • delete — cascade tree: associations cascaded by REMOVE or orphanRemoval are shown in orange and expanded, so a surprise cascading delete is visible before you run it.
  • Skips what loads nothing: count, exists, and scalar / DTO projections show no tree — decided by the method's return type, not its name, so a findBy… that actually runs a count is handled correctly.
  • Reads mappings and query together: @ManyToOne/@OneToOne/@OneToMany/@ManyToMany (Jakarta and javax), @Query join fetch with alias resolution, and @EntityGraph(attributePaths).
  • Flags a pitfall — a non-owning @OneToOne declared LAZY is loaded EAGER by Hibernate, and is marked accordingly.
  • Back-references to an already-loaded parent are shown without color, since navigating to them triggers no extra query.
  • Configurable colors — all five (LAZY / EAGER / FETCH / save cascade / delete cascade) under Settings | Tools | JPA Fetch Lens.

On save, the cascade tree (blue):

On delete, the cascade tree (orange):

Cardinality is shown as N-1, 1-N, 1-1, N-M. Works in IntelliJ IDEA Community and Ultimate; it relies on Java PSI, so no dedicated JPA plugin is required — just a project with JPA or Spring Data JPA on the classpath.

Source code and issue tracker on GitHub
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• 0.2.0 — save and delete now show a cascade tree (what gets persisted / removed along with the entity, including orphanRemoval), not a fetch tree — in their own colors (save = blue, delete = orange by default, both configurable). Method type is decided by return type, so count / exists / scalar and DTO projections correctly show no tree even when named findBy….
  • 0.1.1 — Fix: inherited repository methods (findById, findAll, save, …) now show the fetch tree too. Previously only methods declared directly in the repository interface did.
  • 0.1.0 — Initial release. Per-method fetch tree on hover, color coding by effective fetch, @Query join fetch and @EntityGraph detection, non-owning @OneToOne LAZY pitfall warning, and configurable colors.
